title: Decadent Crispy Uruguayan Onion Empanadas
description: A halal-friendly Uruguayan dish featuring yellow onion, these empanadas are crispy on the outside and fatty on the inside, perfect for a show-off snack. The deglaze adds a rich flavor to the onions.
instructions: Preheat the oil in a deep frying pan to 350 degrees Fahrenheit.
Make the empanada dough by combining flour and water, then knead for 5 minutes.
Let the dough rest for 30 minutes before rolling it out to a thickness of 1/8 inch.
Cut out circles of dough using a cookie cutter or the rim of a glass.
Caramelize the onion in a pan with a little bit of oil over medium heat.
Deglaze the pan with white vinegar to add a rich flavor to the onions.
Place a tablespoon of caramelized onion in the center of each dough circle.
Brush the edges of the dough with egg wash and fold the dough in half to form a half-moon shape.
Press the edges together to seal the empanada.
Fry the empanadas in hot oil until they are golden brown and crispy.
Drain the empanadas on paper towels and serve hot.
